panels.title: Trips stuck past reconcile window (now)
panels.description: In-flight envelope trips created > StaleThresholdHours ago — the reconciler no longer polls RIOT3 for them, so a dropped webhook here never self-heals. Any value > 0 is a real stuck order. Drives the AmrTripsStuckBeyondReconcileWindow alert.

panels.title: Reconcile window: stuck vs in-flight over time
panels.description: stuck = past the window (abandoned). inflight = inside the window, still being reconciled each tick. A rising 'stuck' with flat 'inflight' means trips are aging out unhealed.

panels.title: RIOT3 reconciler fetch errors / min
panels.description: Trips whose RIOT3 fetch failed per minute (timeout / unreachable). Sustained > 0 = vendor connectivity trouble; dropped webhooks won't be healed and trips will start aging toward stuck. Drives the Riot3ReconcilerFetchErrors alert.

panels.title: Trips healed by reconciler / min
panels.description: Trips the reconciler corrected to match RIOT3 state per minute — i.e. dropped webhooks the backstop recovered. A high sustained rate means webhooks are being lost upstream (investigate RIOT3 → DTMS delivery).

panels.title: Vehicles backfilled post-terminal / min
panels.description: Trips whose vendor robot was recovered AFTER the trip reached a terminal state — the TASK_PROCESSING signal was missed, so the vehicle was filled from RIOT3's executeVehicleKey (reconciler terminal pass or self-heal sweep). Distinct from 'healed': that corrects a live-state divergence, this fills a capture that was never made. A sustained rate means the PROCESSING webhook is being dropped upstream (investigate RIOT3 → DTMS delivery).
